task:
You are taking over a Phase 7 stabilization task in the Nimbus repo at /Users/nickdejesus/Code/nimbus.

Context:
- Branch: phase-07
- PR: https://github.com/dayhaysoos/nimbus/pull/10
- Real provider target: Cloudflare Workers Assets
- Worker URL: https://nimbus-worker.ndejesus1227.workers.dev
- Preview worker project currently configured: nimbus-workspace-preview
- Preview domain configured: getnimbus.dev
- We repeatedly got contract/test “passes” on terminal status, but manual E2E showed the real success path does not produce a reachable preview URL.
- We need true end-to-end behavior: create workspace -> deploy -> terminal succeeded -> deployedUrl reachable.


Important current findings:
1) A real manual deploy flow fails today:
   - workspace: ws_zd7a17qq
   - deployment: dep_vxql2gof
   - terminal: failed
   - error: provider_deploy_failed
   - message: “Fallback script update succeeded but preview URL alias is not reachable”
2) The provider fallback path currently uses script update behavior and synthetic/sentinel provider IDs.
3) Core likely product bug:
   - provider currently uploads a tarball output bundle blob instead of publishing expanded static files from deploy.outputDir as assets manifest entries.
   - alias/routing assumptions (`dep-<deploymentId>.<previewDomain>`) are not actually guaranteed by current fallback API flow.
4) We need to stop relying on status-only pass conditions; URL reachability must be part of acceptance.


Your mission:
Implement a robust, real success path that satisfies Phase 7 objective in practice, not just contract shape.


Scope:
- Read spec: specs/phases/07-real-deployment-provider.md
- Read current provider implementation/tests:
  - packages/worker/src/lib/workspace-deploy-provider.ts
  - packages/worker/src/lib/workspace-deploy-provider.test.ts
  - packages/worker/src/lib/workspace-deployment-runner.ts
  - packages/worker/src/lib/workspace-deployment-runner.test.ts
  - scripts/test-cloudflare-contract.mjs
- Keep simulated

Go ahead and implement the plan, however I want you to build in terms of slices/features. After each feature, review for bugs and test. If it fails, fix, repeat. Don’t move on to the next vertical slice or feature until the one you’re currently on is complete
